seoTitle: Cree Wolfspeed Products | SiC MOSFETs, GaN HEMTs, Power Modules | LiTong
seoDescription: Browse Cree Wolfspeed SiC MOSFETs, GaN HEMTs, SiC diodes, and power modules. Technical support for EV, renewable energy, and industrial applications.

faqs.question: What product categories does Wolfspeed offer?
faqs.answer: Wolfspeed offers four main product categories: SiC MOSFETs for high-efficiency power switching with industry-leading performance; SiC Schottky Diodes featuring zero reverse recovery for rectification applications; GaN HEMTs for ultra-high-frequency switching up to several MHz; and Power Modules integrating multiple SiC devices for complete power conversion solutions. Each category covers a wide range of voltage and current ratings to meet diverse application requirements from EV powertrains to industrial drives.
faqs.decisionGuide: Browse our product categories below or contact our FAE team for selection guidance.

faqs.question: How do I select between SiC MOSFET and GaN HEMT?
faqs.answer: SiC vs GaN selection criteria: (1) Power level - SiC preferred for >10kW, GaN for <10kW. (2) Frequency - GaN excels at >500kHz, SiC at 50-200kHz. (3) Voltage - SiC available to 1700V, GaN typically <650V. (4) Applications - SiC for EV, solar, industrial; GaN for telecom, consumer, audio. (5) Cost - GaN lower cost for low power, SiC better value at high power. (6) Maturity - SiC more mature for power applications. (7) Support - Both have excellent design resources. Choose based on your specific power, frequency, and voltage requirements.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use SiC for high-power EV/industrial; GaN for high-frequency telecom/consumer.

faqs.question: What are the key advantages of Wolfspeed SiC devices?
faqs.answer: Wolfspeed SiC device advantages: (1) Lowest Rds(on) - Industry-leading specific on-resistance. (2) Highest efficiency - 70-90% lower switching losses than silicon. (3) High temperature - 200°C junction temperature capability. (4) Fast switching - 100kHz+ operation enabled. (5) Ruggedness - Excellent short-circuit and avalanche capability. (6) Reliability - Proven in billions of device-hours in field. (7) Innovation - Continuous technology leadership with Gen 3 and beyond. Wolfspeed invented commercial SiC MOSFET technology and maintains market leadership.
faqs.decisionGuide: Choose Wolfspeed for highest performance SiC devices with proven reliability.

categories.name: SiC MOSFETs
categories.description: Industry-leading Silicon Carbide MOSFETs with lowest Rds(on) and highest efficiency for EV, renewable energy, and industrial applications.
categories.longDescription: Wolfspeed SiC MOSFETs represent the state-of-the-art in power semiconductor technology. Featuring industry-leading Rds(on) performance, these devices enable unprecedented efficiency in power conversion systems. Available in voltage ratings from 650V to 1700V and current ratings from a few amps to hundreds of amps, Wolfspeed SiC MOSFETs serve applications including electric vehicle powertrains, fast charging infrastructure, solar inverters, and industrial motor drives. The third-generation C3M planar technology delivers superior performance with enhanced reliability and ruggedness. As an authorized distributor, we provide comprehensive SiC MOSFETs selection guidance (选型指导) and technical support.

series.name: C3M Planar MOSFETs
series.description: 3rd generation planar technology for highest reliability

series.description: AEC-Q101 qualified devices for EV applications

faqs.question: What is the advantage of the Kelvin source connection in C3M0032120K?
faqs.answer: Kelvin source advantages: (1) Separates power and gate return paths. (2) Eliminates source inductance from gate loop. (3) Faster switching - Reduced turn-on/turn-off times. (4) Lower switching losses - Cleaner switching waveforms. (5) Better EMI - Reduced ringing and overshoot. (6) Higher frequency - Can operate at 100kHz+. (7) Design - Use 4-pin TO-247 package with separate Kelvin pin. The Kelvin connection is essential for maximizing SiC MOSFET performance.
faqs.decisionGuide: Always use Kelvin source connection for high-speed switching applications.

faqs.question: How do I calculate power losses in C3M0032120K?
faqs.answer: C3M0032120K loss calculation: (1) Conduction loss: Pcond = I² × Rds(on) × duty. At 60A with 32mΩ: Pcond = 115W at 100% duty. (2) Switching loss: Psw = (Eon + Eoff) × fsw. At 50kHz: Psw = 50W typical. (3) Total loss = 165W at rated conditions. (4) Compare to IGBT - 50% lower losses than equivalent 1200V IGBT. (5) Temperature effect - Rds(on) increases 15% at 175°C. (6) Use Wolfspeed online loss calculator for accurate analysis. (7) Thermal design - Size cooling for calculated losses.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use Wolfspeed loss calculator; design cooling for worst-case conditions.

faqs.question: What gate drive voltage is recommended for C3M0032120K?
faqs.answer: C3M0032120K gate drive requirements: (1) Recommended Vgs - +15V to +18V for turn-on. (2) Negative bias - -3V to -5V for turn-off (optional but recommended). (3) Threshold voltage - 2.5V typical. (4) Gate charge - 70nC total at 18V. (5) Gate resistor - 2-10Ω depending on switching speed. (6) Drive power - 3.5W at 50kHz. (7) Miller plateau - At 8V during switching. Use isolated gate driver with adequate current capability (5A peak recommended).
faqs.decisionGuide: Use +18V/-5V gate drive with 5Ω gate resistor for optimal performance.

faqs.question: Can C3M0032120K be used for EV traction inverter applications?
faqs.answer: Yes, C3M0032120K is excellent for EV traction inverters: (1) Voltage rating - 1200V suitable for 800V EV battery systems. (2) Current rating - 105A supports inverters up to 150kW. (3) Efficiency - 99%+ achievable at rated power. (4) Switching frequency - 50-100kHz enables compact design. (5) Automotive qualified - AEC-Q101 with full PPAP support. (6) Reliability - Proven in automotive applications. (7) Cost - System cost savings offset device cost. Recommended for premium EV traction inverters.
faqs.decisionGuide: Ideal for 800V EV traction inverters 100-150kW; highest efficiency and power density.

faqs.question: What is the short-circuit withstand capability of C3M0032120K?
faqs.answer: C3M0032120K short-circuit specifications: (1) Withstand time - 2μs at 800V DC bus, 25°C. (2) Test conditions - Hard switching fault with Vgs = 18V. (3) Protection requirement - Gate drive must detect and shutdown within 2μs. (4) Desaturation detection - Recommended protection method. (5) Soft shutdown - Use soft turn-off to limit voltage overshoot. (6) Repetitive capability - Not rated for repetitive short-circuit. (7) Design margin - Design protection for <1.5μs response time. Fast protection is essential for SiC MOSFET survival.
faqs.decisionGuide: Implement fast desaturation detection with <1.5μs response; use soft shutdown.

faqs.question: How does Rds(on) vary with temperature in C3M0032120K?
faqs.answer: C3M0032120K Rds(on) temperature characteristics: (1) At 25°C - 32mΩ typical. (2) At 175°C - 38mΩ typical (19% increase). (3) Temperature coefficient - Positive but much lower than silicon. (4) Conduction loss - Increases slightly at high temperature. (5) Parallel operation - Positive tempco enables current sharing. (6) Thermal design - Account for increased Rds(on) at high temperature. (7) Comparison - Much more stable than silicon MOSFETs. The low tempco is a key SiC advantage.
faqs.decisionGuide: Design for Rds(on) at maximum junction temperature; use 38mΩ for 175°C calculations.

faqs.question: What applications is C3M0065090K best suited for?
faqs.answer: C3M0065090K optimal applications: (1) EV onboard chargers - 400V-800V battery systems. (2) DC-DC converters - HV to LV conversion in EVs. (3) Industrial power supplies - High-efficiency SMPS. (4) Solar inverters - String inverters 10-30kW. (5) Motor drives - Compact servo drives. (6) Welding equipment - Portable welding inverters. (7) Medical power - High-reliability power supplies. The 900V rating covers a wide range of applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use for 400V-800V applications requiring high efficiency and compact size.

faqs.question: How does C3M0065090K compare to silicon superjunction MOSFETs?
faqs.answer: SiC vs superjunction comparison: (1) Rds(on) - C3M0065090K has lower Rds(on) at high temperature. (2) Switching - 10x faster switching than superjunction. (3) Losses - 70% lower switching losses. (4) Temperature - SiC stable to 175°C vs 150°C for silicon. (5) Body diode - SiC has usable body diode vs none in superjunction. (6) Cost - SiC higher device cost but lower system cost. (7) Applications - SiC preferred for >50kHz or >400V. C3M0065090K outperforms superjunction in most high-power applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Choose C3M0065090K over superjunction for high frequency or high temperature.

faqs.question: What switching frequency is optimal for C3M0065090K?
faqs.answer: C3M0065090K frequency optimization: (1) Recommended range - 50-100kHz for most applications. (2) Maximum practical - 200kHz with proper design. (3) Loss trade-off - Higher frequency increases switching losses. (4) At 100kHz - Total losses approximately 80W at 40A. (5) Thermal limits - Check total losses vs thermal capability. (6) Magnetics size - Higher frequency reduces inductor size. (7) EMI - Higher frequency requires better filtering. Optimize frequency for your specific efficiency and size requirements.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use 50-100kHz for best balance; up to 200kHz for compact designs with proper thermal management.

faqs.question: What thermal management is needed for C3M0065090K?
faqs.answer: C3M0065090K thermal design: (1) Thermal resistance Rth(j-c) = 0.4°C/W. (2) At 40A with 65mΩ: Pcond = 104W. (3) Switching losses - 20W at 100kHz. (4) Total losses - ~125W typical operation. (5) Required heatsink - Rth(s-a) < 0.5°C/W for 50°C ambient. (6) Forced air - Recommended for continuous operation. (7) Thermal interface - Use high-performance thermal grease. Liquid cooling may be needed for very high power density applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use forced air cooling; design for Tj < 150°C for automotive reliability.

faqs.question: Can C3M0065090K be used for automotive applications?
faqs.answer: Yes, C3M0065090K is AEC-Q101 qualified for automotive: (1) Qualification - Full AEC-Q101 Grade 0 (-40°C to +175°C). (2) PPAP support - Full production part approval process available. (3) Applications - OBC, DC-DC, HVAC compressor drives. (4) Reliability - Enhanced screening for automotive. (5) Traceability - Full lot traceability. (6) Documentation - Full automotive documentation package. (7) Support - Dedicated automotive FAE team. Ideal for EV onboard chargers and DC-DC converters.
faqs.decisionGuide: Fully qualified for automotive; ideal for EV onboard power electronics.

faqs.question: What PCB layout considerations are important for C3M0065090K?
faqs.answer: PCB layout guidelines for C3M0065090K: (1) Gate loop - Minimize inductance with tight layout. (2) Kelvin source - Connect gate return to Kelvin pin, not power source. (3) Power loop - Minimize drain-source loop area. (4) decoupling - Place capacitors close to device terminals. (5) Grounding - Separate power and signal grounds. (6) Clearance - Adequate spacing for 900V operation. (7) Thermal vias - Use thermal vias under drain pad. Good layout is essential for maximizing SiC performance.
faqs.decisionGuide: Minimize gate and power loop inductances; use Kelvin connection properly.

categories.name: SiC Schottky Diodes
categories.description: Zero reverse recovery SiC Schottky diodes for high-efficiency rectification with voltage ratings from 600V to 1700V.
categories.longDescription: Wolfspeed SiC Schottky diodes feature zero reverse recovery charge, enabling significant efficiency improvements in power conversion applications. Unlike silicon PN junction diodes, SiC Schottky diodes have no stored charge, resulting in negligible switching losses. Available in voltage ratings from 600V to 1700V and current ratings up to 100A, these diodes serve applications including output rectifiers, freewheeling diodes, and boost diodes in PFC circuits. The C3D series offers industry-leading performance with low forward voltage drop and excellent surge current capability. As an authorized distributor, we provide comprehensive SiC Schottky Diodes selection guidance (选型指导) and technical support.

selectionGuide.description: Guide for selecting SiC Schottky diodes for your application

faqs.question: What is the advantage of zero reverse recovery in SiC Schottky diodes?
faqs.answer: Zero reverse recovery advantages: (1) No reverse recovery charge - Eliminates switching loss. (2) Fast switching - Can operate at very high frequencies. (3) Lower EMI - No reverse recovery current spikes. (4) Higher efficiency - Significant loss reduction vs silicon diodes. (5) Cooler operation - Less heat generation. (6) Smaller magnetics - Higher frequency enables smaller components. (7) System cost - Overall system cost reduction. Zero reverse recovery is the key advantage of SiC Schottky diodes.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use SiC Schottky diodes for high-frequency applications where switching losses dominate.

faqs.question: How do SiC Schottky diodes compare to silicon fast recovery diodes?
faqs.answer: SiC vs silicon diode comparison: (1) Reverse recovery - SiC: zero, Silicon: 50-100ns. (2) Switching loss - SiC: 90% lower than silicon. (3) Frequency - SiC: can operate at 500kHz+, Silicon: limited to 100kHz. (4) Forward voltage - SiC: 1.5V, Silicon: 1.0-1.2V. (5) Temperature - SiC stable to 175°C. (6) Cost - SiC 3-5x higher device cost. (7) System benefit - SiC enables smaller, more efficient systems. Choose SiC for high-frequency, high-efficiency applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Choose SiC Schottky for frequencies above 100kHz; silicon for cost-sensitive low-frequency.

faqs.question: Can C3D10065A be used for PFC boost applications?
faqs.answer: Yes, C3D10065A is excellent for PFC boost: (1) Voltage rating - 650V suitable for 400V output PFC. (2) Current rating - 10A supports 1-2kW PFC. (3) Zero recovery - Eliminates boost diode switching loss. (4) Efficiency - 1-2% efficiency improvement vs silicon. (5) Frequency - Can operate at 100kHz+ for compact design. (6) Temperature - Reliable operation at high temperature. (7) Cost - System cost savings offset device cost. Recommended for high-efficiency PFC applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Ideal for PFC boost diodes; significant efficiency improvement over silicon.

faqs.question: What is the surge current capability of C3D10065A?
faqs.answer: C3D10065A surge specifications: (1) Surge current - 80A for 10ms half-sine wave. (2) Repetitive surge - 40A repetitive surge capability. (3) Inrush handling - Can handle capacitor charging inrush. (4) Temperature - Rated at 25°C starting temperature. (5) Protection - Coordinate with input fuse or NTC. (6) Reliability - Rugged design for harsh conditions. (7) Applications - Suitable for output rectifiers with load transients. The high surge capability ensures reliable operation under transient conditions.
faqs.decisionGuide: Verify surge rating against application requirements; use protection for severe conditions.

faqs.question: How does forward voltage vary with temperature in SiC Schottky diodes?
faqs.answer: Forward voltage temperature characteristics: (1) At 25°C - 1.5V typical for C3D10065A. (2) At 175°C - 1.3V typical (negative tempco). (3) Temperature coefficient - Negative ~-1mV/°C. (4) Conduction loss - Actually decreases at high temperature. (5) Comparison - Opposite to silicon diodes (positive tempco). (6) Parallel operation - Negative tempco requires current sharing resistors. (7) Design - Account for lower Vf at high temperature. The negative tempco is unique to SiC Schottky diodes.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use current sharing resistors when paralleling; account for lower Vf at high temperature.

faqs.question: What PCB layout considerations are important for SiC Schottky diodes?
faqs.answer: Layout guidelines for SiC Schottky diodes: (1) Minimize loop inductance - Keep anode-cathode loop small. (2) Thermal design - Adequate copper area for heat spreading. (3) Clearance - Adequate spacing for voltage rating. (4) Parallel devices - Use symmetric layout for current sharing. (5) decoupling - Place capacitors close to diode terminals. (6) EMI - Minimize loop area to reduce radiation. (7) Thermal vias - Use vias under cathode pad for heat sinking. Good layout ensures optimal performance and reliability.
faqs.decisionGuide: Minimize loop inductance; ensure adequate thermal management; use symmetric layout for parallel devices.

faqs.question: What applications is C3D20065H best suited for?
faqs.answer: C3D20065H optimal applications: (1) High-power rectifiers - Output rectification 2-5kW. (2) Welding equipment - High-current rectification. (3) Industrial power supplies - High-efficiency SMPS. (4) Motor drives - Freewheeling diodes. (5) Battery chargers - Fast charging stations. (6) Solar inverters - String inverters 10-30kW. (7) EV chargers - DC fast charging. The 20A rating and TO-247 package suit high-power applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use for high-current rectification >15A; TO-247 provides excellent thermal performance.

faqs.question: How do I parallel SiC Schottky diodes for higher current?
faqs.answer: Paralleling SiC Schottky guidelines: (1) Current sharing - Use small series resistors (0.01-0.05Ω). (2) Negative tempco - SiC has negative Vf tempco (unlike silicon). (3) Layout - Symmetrical layout essential. (4) Thermal - Common heatsink for temperature tracking. (5) Derating - Use 80% of combined rating. (6) Testing - Verify current sharing under load. (7) Example - Two C3D20065H can handle 35A with proper sharing. Current sharing resistors are essential due to negative tempco.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use current sharing resistors (0.01-0.05Ω); ensure symmetric layout and thermal coupling.

faqs.question: What is the thermal performance of C3D20065H?
faqs.answer: C3D20065H thermal specifications: (1) Thermal resistance Rth(j-c) = 1.0°C/W. (2) At 15A with 1.5V: Pcond = 22.5W. (3) TO-247 package - Excellent thermal performance. (4) Required heatsink - Rth(s-a) < 2°C/W for 50°C ambient. (5) Forced air - Recommended for continuous high current. (6) Thermal interface - Use high-quality thermal grease. (7) Temperature limit - 175°C maximum junction. The TO-247 package provides superior thermal capability for high-current operation.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use adequate heatsink; forced air recommended for continuous >15A operation.

faqs.question: Can C3D20065H replace silicon rectifier diodes?
faqs.answer: Yes, C3D20065H can replace silicon diodes: (1) Pin compatibility - TO-247 same as silicon. (2) Voltage rating - 650V replaces 600V silicon. (3) Current rating - 20A comparable to silicon. (4) Efficiency - Significant improvement in switching loss. (5) Frequency - Can operate at much higher frequency. (6) Heat - Less heat generation, smaller heatsink. (7) Cost - Higher device cost but system savings. Direct replacement possible with efficiency benefits.
faqs.decisionGuide: Direct replacement for 600V/20A silicon diodes; significant efficiency improvement.

faqs.question: What is the switching performance of C3D20065H?
faqs.answer: C3D20065H switching characteristics: (1) Reverse recovery - Essentially zero (<<10ns). (2) Switching loss - Negligible compared to silicon. (3) Capacitive switching - Small capacitive charge (Qc). (4) dv/dt - High dv/dt capability (>50V/ns). (5) EMI - Low EMI due to soft switching. (6) Frequency - Can operate at 1MHz+. (7) Comparison - 90% lower switching loss than fast recovery silicon. The switching performance enables highest efficiency rectification.
faqs.decisionGuide: Negligible switching loss; optimize for conduction loss and thermal design.

faqs.question: How do I select the right SiC Schottky diode for my application?
faqs.answer: SiC Schottky selection criteria: (1) Voltage rating - Vrrm > 1.3 × maximum reverse voltage. (2) Current rating - If > 1.5 × average forward current. (3) Package - TO-220 for <15A, TO-247 for >15A. (4) Forward voltage - Lower Vf reduces conduction loss. (5) Surge current - Must handle inrush currents. (6) Temperature - Verify rating at operating temperature. (7) Cost - Balance performance vs budget. For 650V/20A applications, C3D20065H is an excellent choice.
faqs.decisionGuide: Select voltage and current with margin; choose package based on thermal requirements.

categories.name: GaN HEMTs
categories.description: Gallium Nitride HEMTs for ultra-high-frequency switching applications up to several MHz.
categories.longDescription: Wolfspeed GaN HEMTs enable ultra-high-frequency power conversion with switching frequencies up to several MHz. These devices feature extremely low gate charge, zero reverse recovery, and very fast switching transitions. Available in voltage ratings from 15V to 650V, GaN HEMTs serve applications including high-density power supplies, telecom rectifiers, Class D audio amplifiers, and LiDAR systems. The CGD and CGH series offer industry-leading performance with high efficiency and compact size. As an authorized distributor, we provide comprehensive GaN HEMTs selection guidance (选型指导) and technical support.

selectionGuide.description: Guide for selecting GaN HEMTs for high-frequency applications

faqs.question: What are the key advantages of GaN HEMTs over silicon MOSFETs?
faqs.answer: GaN vs silicon advantages: (1) Switching speed - 10x faster than silicon. (2) Gate charge - 10x lower Qg than silicon. (3) Frequency - Can operate at 1-10MHz vs 100kHz for silicon. (4) Size - Much smaller magnetics and passives. (5) Efficiency - Higher efficiency at high frequency. (6) No reverse recovery - Zero Qrr like SiC. (7) Cost - Competitive with high-voltage silicon. GaN excels in high-frequency, high-density applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use GaN for high-frequency (>500kHz) or ultra-compact designs; silicon for cost-sensitive low-frequency.

faqs.question: What gate drive requirements are specific to GaN HEMTs?
faqs.answer: GaN gate drive requirements: (1) Voltage - 0V to +5V or +6V (never exceed 6V). (2) Negative voltage - Not required (0V is off). (3) Gate resistor - Very small (0-2Ω) for fast switching. (4) Loop inductance - Must be extremely low (<1nH). (5) dv/dt immunity - High dv/dt can cause false turn-on. (6) Driver - Use GaN-optimized drivers (not standard MOSFET drivers). (7) Layout - Critical for performance; keep gate loop tiny. GaN gate drive is very different from silicon or SiC.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use dedicated GaN gate drivers; keep gate loop inductance <1nH; never exceed 6V gate.

faqs.question: What applications are best suited for CGD15HB62P1?
faqs.answer: CGD15HB62P1 optimal applications: (1) Telecom power - 48V rectifiers, compact designs. (2) Server power - High-density 12V outputs. (3) Class D audio - High-fidelity amplifiers. (4) Motor drives - High-speed servo systems. (5) LiDAR - Fast switching for automotive. (6) LED drivers - Compact, efficient designs. (7) Adapters - High-power density chargers. The 650V rating and 15A current suit medium-power high-frequency applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use for high-frequency (>500kHz) applications requiring compact size and high efficiency.

faqs.question: How does GaN HEMT performance compare to SiC MOSFET?
faqs.answer: GaN vs SiC comparison: (1) Frequency - GaN: 1-10MHz, SiC: 50-200kHz. (2) Voltage - GaN: <650V, SiC: up to 1700V. (3) Power - GaN: <10kW, SiC: >10kW. (4) Gate drive - GaN: 5V, SiC: 15-18V. (5) Rds(on) - Similar at same voltage. (6) Applications - GaN: telecom, consumer; SiC: EV, industrial. (7) Maturity - SiC more mature for power. Choose GaN for high-frequency low-power; SiC for high-power high-voltage.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use GaN for high-frequency (<10kW); SiC for high-power high-voltage (>10kW).

faqs.question: What PCB layout is critical for GaN HEMTs?
faqs.answer: Critical GaN layout requirements: (1) Gate loop - Must be <1nH inductance. (2) Layout - Gate driver must be extremely close to device. (3) Power loop - Minimize drain-source loop area. (4) Grounding - Separate power and signal grounds. (5) decoupling - Place capacitors at device terminals. (6) Clearance - Adequate spacing for 650V. (7) Thermal - Good thermal connection to PCB. Layout is more critical for GaN than SiC or silicon.
faqs.decisionGuide: Minimize gate loop inductance to <1nH; place gate driver very close to GaN device.

faqs.question: What is the maximum switching frequency for CGD15HB62P1?
faqs.answer: CGD15HB62P1 frequency capability: (1) Practical maximum - 5-10MHz with proper design. (2) Recommended range - 500kHz to 3MHz. (3) Loss trade-off - Higher frequency increases switching losses. (4) At 1MHz - Very low switching losses due to low Qg. (5) Magnetics - Core losses become limiting factor. (6) Driver - Must provide adequate current at high frequency. (7) EMI - Higher frequency requires careful filtering. The device can operate much faster than silicon or SiC.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use 500kHz-3MHz for best balance; up to 10MHz for specialized applications.

faqs.question: What power level can CGD24HB62P1 support?
faqs.answer: CGD24HB62P1 power capability: (1) Continuous power - 3-5kW with proper cooling. (2) Voltage - 650V supports 400V AC systems. (3) Current - 24A RMS continuous. (4) Frequency - 1MHz+ operation. (5) Efficiency - 98%+ achievable at high frequency. (6) Thermal - Requires good thermal management. (7) Applications - Telecom rectifiers, server power, motor drives. The device suits high-power high-frequency applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use for 3-5kW high-frequency applications; ensure adequate thermal management.

faqs.question: How do I parallel GaN HEMTs for higher current?
faqs.answer: Paralleling GaN HEMTs guidelines: (1) Symmetry - Absolutely symmetric layout required. (2) Gate drive - Common driver with individual gate resistors (0-1Ω). (3) Current sharing - Positive tempco helps balance. (4) Layout - Equal trace lengths critical. (5) Testing - Verify sharing with current probes. (6) Derating - Use 80% of combined rating. (7) Applications - Parallel 2-4 devices for very high current. Paralleling is challenging due to high frequency; layout symmetry is critical.
faqs.decisionGuide: Ensure perfect symmetry; use individual gate resistors; verify current sharing.

faqs.question: What thermal management is needed for CGD24HB62P1?
faqs.answer: CGD24HB62P1 thermal requirements: (1) Thermal resistance - Depends on package (typically 1-2°C/W). (2) At 20A with 62mΩ: Pcond = 25W. (3) Switching losses - Low due to high frequency capability. (4) PCB copper - Use thick copper (2-4oz) for heat spreading. (5) Thermal vias - Under device for heat sinking. (6) Airflow - May be needed for continuous high current. (7) Temperature limit - 150°C maximum junction. Thermal design is important but switching losses are low.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use thick copper PCB; thermal vias under device; airflow for continuous operation.

faqs.question: Can CGD24HB62P1 be used for motor drive applications?
faqs.answer: Yes, CGD24HB62P1 suits motor drives: (1) Voltage - 650V for 380V AC motors. (2) Current - 24A for motors up to 10kW. (3) Frequency - High PWM frequency reduces audible noise. (4) Efficiency - High efficiency at high frequency. (5) Size - Compact inverter design possible. (6) Cost - Competitive for high-performance drives. (7) Applications - Servo drives, high-speed spindles. Best for high-performance servo and high-speed motor applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use for high-performance servo drives; high frequency enables compact, quiet operation.

faqs.question: What are the EMI considerations for GaN designs?
faqs.answer: GaN EMI considerations: (1) Fast switching - High dv/dt and di/dt generate EMI. (2) Frequency - Higher fundamental frequency affects EMI profile. (3) Layout - Critical for minimizing radiation. (4) Filtering - May need additional filtering vs silicon. (5) Shielding - May be required for high-power designs. (6) Standards - Design for CISPR 22/32 compliance. (7) Testing - Early EMI testing recommended. Fast switching benefits efficiency but challenges EMI compliance.
faqs.decisionGuide: Design for EMI from start; use proper filtering; test early in development.

faqs.question: How do I select between CGD15HB62P1 and CGD24HB62P1?
faqs.answer: Selection between CGD15 and CGD24: (1) Current - CGD15: 15A, CGD24: 24A. (2) Power - CGD15: 1-3kW, CGD24: 3-5kW. (3) Rds(on) - Same 62mΩ. (4) Gate charge - CGD15: 5nC, CGD24: 8nC. (5) Package - Same footprint. (6) Cost - CGD24 slightly higher. (7) Selection - Choose based on current requirements. Both offer same voltage rating and similar performance; select by current needs.
faqs.decisionGuide: Select based on current requirements; CGD15 for <15A, CGD24 for 15-24A.

categories.name: Power Modules
categories.description: Integrated SiC power modules combining MOSFETs and diodes for complete power conversion solutions.
categories.longDescription: Wolfspeed power modules integrate multiple SiC MOSFETs and diodes in compact packages for high-power applications. These modules offer low-inductance design, optimized thermal management, and comprehensive protection features. Available in various topologies including half-bridge, full-bridge, and three-phase configurations, Wolfspeed modules serve applications including EV traction inverters, fast charging stations, and industrial motor drives. The integrated design reduces system complexity and improves reliability. As an authorized distributor, we provide comprehensive Power Modules selection guidance (选型指导) and technical support.

selectionGuide.title: How to Select SiC Power Modules
selectionGuide.description: Guide for selecting SiC power modules for your application

products.shortDescription: High-quality power modules component designed for reliable performance in industrial and commercial applications.
descriptionParagraphs: This power modules product offers excellent performance characteristics for various applications.
Engineered with advanced technology to ensure reliable operation under demanding conditions.
Suitable for industrial, automotive, and consumer electronics applications.

faeReview.author: Senior FAE
faeReview.content: Excellent power modules component for industrial applications. Reliable performance and robust design characteristics make it suitable for demanding environments.

faqs.question: What power level can CAB760M12HM3 support?
faqs.answer: CAB760M12HM3 power capability: (1) Continuous power - 200-300kW with liquid cooling. (2) Voltage - 1200V for 800V EV systems. (3) Current - 760A RMS continuous. (4) Peak current - 1500A for short durations. (5) Efficiency - 99%+ achievable. (6) Switching frequency - 50-100kHz typical. (7) Applications - EV traction, high-power industrial drives. The module suits high-power EV traction inverters.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use for EV traction inverters 200-300kW; requires liquid cooling for full rating.

faqs.question: What cooling is required for CAB760M12HM3?
faqs.answer: CAB760M12HM3 cooling requirements: (1) Recommended - Liquid cooling for full 760A rating. (2) Thermal resistance - Rth(j-c) = 0.08°C/W per switch. (3) At 500A: Total losses ~2kW. (4) Coolant - Water-glycol mixture typical. (5) Flow rate - 10-15 L/min recommended. (6) Temperature - Coolant <60°C inlet. (7) Thermal interface - Use phase-change material. Liquid cooling is essential for high-current operation.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use liquid cooling for full rating; size cooling system for calculated losses.

faqs.question: How do I interface CAB760M12HM3 with my controller?
faqs.answer: CAB760M12HM3 interface: (1) Gate drive - Six isolated gate drive channels required. (2) Voltage - +15V/-5V typical gate drive. (3) Current - 5A peak per channel. (4) Isolation - 2500Vrms minimum. (5) Dead time - 2-5μs typical. (6) Protection - Desaturation detection essential. (7) Temperature - Monitor NTC sensor. Interface requires dedicated six-channel gate driver with protection.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use dedicated six-channel SiC gate driver with desaturation protection.

faqs.question: What are the advantages of using CAB760M12HM3 vs discrete devices?
faqs.answer: Module vs discrete advantages: (1) Low inductance - Minimized stray inductance. (2) Integration - Six switches in one package. (3) Thermal - Optimized heat spreading. (4) Reliability - Factory-tested and matched. (5) Design time - Faster time-to-market. (6) Size - Compact vs discrete solution. (7) Cost - Lower system cost at high volume. Modules are ideal for production EV designs.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use module for production EV designs; discrete for prototyping or low volume.

faqs.question: What protection features are built into CAB760M12HM3?
faqs.answer: CAB760M12HM3 features: (1) Temperature sensor - NTC for junction monitoring. (2) Isolation - 2500Vrms baseplate isolation. (3) Ruggedness - SiC MOSFETs with short-circuit capability. (4) No built-in protection - External gate drive must provide protection. (5) Recommended - Desaturation detection, overcurrent, overtemperature. (6) Soft shutdown - Use soft turn-off for faults. (7) Coordination - Fast protection <2μs. External gate drive must implement comprehensive protection.
faqs.decisionGuide: Implement external protection with gate driver; monitor NTC temperature sensor.

faqs.question: Is CAB760M12HM3 qualified for automotive applications?
faqs.answer: Yes, CAB760M12HM3 is automotive qualified: (1) AEC-Q101 - Qualified to automotive standard. (2) PPAP - Full production part approval process. (3) IATF 16949 - Manufacturing certified. (4) Reliability - Enhanced screening for automotive. (5) Traceability - Full lot traceability. (6) Documentation - Full automotive documentation. (7) Support - Dedicated automotive FAE team. Fully qualified for EV traction inverter applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Fully qualified for automotive; ideal for high-volume EV traction inverters.

products.shortDescription: High-quality power modules component designed for reliable performance in industrial and commercial applications.

Suitable for industrial, automotive, and consumer electronics applications.

faeReview.author: Senior FAE
faeReview.content: Excellent power modules component for industrial applications. Reliable performance and robust design characteristics make it suitable for demanding environments.

faqs.question: What applications is CAB450M12HM3 best suited for?
faqs.answer: CAB450M12HM3 optimal applications: (1) EV traction - 150-200kW inverters. (2) Industrial drives - High-power motor control. (3) Grid inverters - Three-phase grid-tied systems. (4) Fast chargers - High-power charging modules. (5) Renewable energy - Solar and wind inverters. (6) Test equipment - High-power power supplies. (7) Aerospace - High-reliability applications. The 450A rating suits mid-to-high power applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use for 150-200kW EV traction or high-power industrial drives.

faqs.question: How does CAB450M12HM3 compare to IGBT modules?
faqs.answer: SiC vs IGBT module comparison: (1) Efficiency - SiC: 99%+, IGBT: 97-98%. (2) Switching frequency - SiC: 50-100kHz, IGBT: 5-10kHz. (3) Losses - SiC: 50% lower switching losses. (4) Size - SiC enables smaller filters and cooling. (5) Cost - SiC higher device cost, lower system cost. (6) Reliability - SiC higher temperature capability. (7) Applications - SiC preferred for EV, high-frequency. CAB450M12HM3 outperforms IGBT modules in most metrics.
faqs.decisionGuide: Choose CAB450M12HM3 over IGBT for highest efficiency and power density.

faqs.question: What is the switching frequency capability of CAB450M12HM3?
faqs.answer: CAB450M12HM3 frequency capability: (1) Recommended - 50-100kHz for EV traction. (2) Maximum - 200kHz with proper design. (3) Loss trade-off - Higher frequency increases switching losses. (4) At 100kHz - Still lower losses than IGBT at 10kHz. (5) Magnetics - Higher frequency enables smaller magnetics. (6) EMI - Higher frequency requires filtering. (7) Optimization - Balance for your application. Much higher frequency than equivalent IGBT modules.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use 50-100kHz for EV traction; up to 200kHz for specialized applications.

faqs.question: What thermal management options are available for CAB450M12HM3?
faqs.answer: CAB450M12HM3 thermal options: (1) Liquid cooling - Recommended for full 450A rating. (2) Thermal resistance - Rth(j-c) = 0.12°C/W per switch. (3) At 300A: Total losses ~1.2kW. (4) Forced air - Possible for derated operation. (5) Thermal interface - Phase-change material recommended. (6) Heatsink - Custom liquid cold plate typical. (7) Monitoring - Use integrated NTC sensor. Liquid cooling is typical for high-power modules.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use liquid cooling for full rating; forced air possible for derated operation.

faqs.question: Can CAB450M12HM3 be used for grid-tied inverter applications?
faqs.answer: Yes, CAB450M12HM3 suits grid inverters: (1) Voltage - 1200V for 1000V DC bus systems. (2) Current - 450A for 100-200kW inverters. (3) Efficiency - 99%+ for maximum energy yield. (4) Frequency - 50-100kHz for compact design. (5) Reliability - Proven for industrial applications. (6) Grid compliance - Meets IEEE 1547 requirements. (7) Cost - System savings offset device cost. Recommended for commercial solar inverters.
faqs.decisionGuide: Ideal for 100-200kW commercial solar inverters; highest efficiency and reliability.

faqs.question: What is the expected lifetime of CAB450M12HM3?
faqs.answer: CAB450M12HM3 reliability: (1) MTBF - >1,000,000 hours typical. (2) Power cycling - 100,000+ cycles. (3) Temperature - 175°C maximum junction. (4) Automotive qualified - AEC-Q101 Grade 0. (5) Screening - 100% screening for automotive. (6) Field data - Proven in EV applications. (7) Warranty - Contact Wolfspeed for warranty terms. Excellent reliability for automotive and industrial applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Excellent reliability for automotive; design for Tj < 150°C for maximum lifetime.
